name: NextGen Healthcare EHR & Practice Management API
what_it_does: NextGen Healthcare EHR REST and FHIR API for ambulatory care electronic health records and practice management platform. Enables AI agents to manage patient demographic and clinical record access via FHIR R4, handle appointment scheduling and provider availability queries, access clinical documentation and encounter data for care coordination, retrieve lab orders and results for diagnostic workflow automation, manage prescription and medication history for pharmacy integrations, handle billing and claims data for revenue cycle management, access population health and care gap analytics, retrieve quality measure and HEDIS reporting data for value-based care, manage patient portal and engagement automation, and integrate EHR data with analytics, RCM, and population health platforms.
use_cases: Accessing patient FHIR records for care coordination agents
Automating appointment scheduling from patient engagement agents
Retrieving billing and claims data from RCM automation agents
Monitoring care gaps from population health agents
not_for: Large hospital systems without ambulatory specialty or multi-specialty group focus
Consumer health apps without provider-facing clinical workflow context
Non-clinical analytics without EHR data access requirements
best_when: A specialty or multi-specialty ambulatory practice using NextGen EHR wants AI agents to automate clinical data access, scheduling, billing workflows, and FHIR-based interoperability.
avoid_when: HIPAA RISK: EHR data requires HIPAA BAA and patient authorization; AI clinical decision tools require FDA SaMD review. Automated scheduling or billing changes can impact patient care and revenue — require human approval workflows. Practice management automation without provider review can cause scheduling conflicts and billing errors.

auth.notes: NextGen uses SMART on FHIR OAuth 2.0 for FHIR R4 endpoints. Legacy NextGen API uses session-based token auth. Developer portal at developer.nextgen.com. ONC-certified FHIR R4 API for 21st Century Cures compliance. HIPAA BAA required for production data access. Vendor-facing APIs for certified third-party applications via App Development Program.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer sandbox for certified partners; production requires customer relationship
pricing.paid_tiers: API access with EHR subscription; App Development Program for third-party integrators
pricing.notes: Horsham, Pennsylvania. Founded 1974. Public company (NXGN). Ambulatory specialty EHR market. Strong in behavioral health, orthopedics, and OB/GYN. 155,000+ providers. Revenue cycle management integrated with EHR. Competes with Allscripts/Veradigm and athenahealth for ambulatory specialty EHR.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: HIPAA BAA required — production clinical data requires signed BAA and patient authorization under 21st Century Cures Act
SMART on FHIR required — new integrations should use FHIR R4 SMART launch; legacy NextGen API endpoints are deprecated path
Certified app program — third-party integrators must complete App Development Program certification before production access
Specialty-specific workflows — NextGen has specialty-specific clinical templates; generic EHR API patterns may miss specialty data structures
RCM integration complexity — billing and claims automation requires understanding of NextGen's Revenue Cycle workflow; errors cause claim rejections
Patient portal separate — NextGen patient portal (Healow) has separate API surface from provider-facing EHR API
agent_readiness.security_notes: Ambulatory specialty EHR. HIPAA, SOC2, ONC. SMART on FHIR OAuth2. US. Clinical records and practice management data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Specialty EHR data. HIPAA, ONC. SMART on FHIR. US. Clinical and practice management data.
